review_notes: The functions generally map well to the control's requirement to update malicious code protection mechanisms. The naming convention is mostly followed, but a slight adjustment was made for clarity and consistency. The functions are technically accurate in representing Kubernetes security practices related to image scanning and admission control.
naming_improvements: Changed 'enabled' to 'applied' in k8s_admission_controller_security_updates_enabled to better reflect the action of applying updates.
title: 3_14_4: 3.14.4 Update malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available
step1_reasoning: The control focuses on updating malicious code protection mechanisms, which in Kubernetes can be mapped to ensuring that container images are scanned for vulnerabilities and that security updates are applied. This can be achieved through image vulnerability scanning and admission controllers that enforce security policies on image updates.
